element_introduction_order.mastery_context: player learns basic batting and fielding
runs and points accumulate through gameplay
advancing through successive opponents increases difficulty

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: turn-based with alternating batting pressure phases; tension builds across innings as score differential changes; 7-inning structure (US version) provides natural arc with climactic final innings

difficulty_curve.early_game: introductory opponents in early tournament rounds establish baseline gameplay mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: mid-bracket opponents increase fielding difficulty and pitcher control challenge
difficulty_curve.late_game: championship series opponents feature faster pitch speeds and more aggressive AI batting

notable_level_patterns.description: Each game follows inning-based progression (7 innings in US version) with alternating offensive/defensive phases; player bats then opponent bats per inning, creating predictable rhythm
Sequential opponent selection through single-elimination or round-robin tournament structure; each win opens access to next opponent; visual bracket shows remaining teams
Japanese version includes ceremonial opening with rock-paper-scissors for first-at-bat determination; US version uses bat-grab mechanic for functional equivalent
